headline: Suaram: Up to 28% of SOSMA Detainees Released Without Charge, From Nearly 7,000 Arrests Since 2018
context: Suaram's 2025 report documented 6,892 SOSMA arrests between 2018 and 2023. Up to 28% were eventually released without being charged — the 28-day no-charge detention is used at scale on people never prosecuted.

cards.big: Suaram: 6,892 SOSMA arrests in 5 years — up to 28% released without ever being charged.
cards.sub: Of arrests under the Security Offences (Special Measures) Act 2012 between 2018 and 2023, roughly one in four did not result in any charge being filed at all.

cards.big: SOSMA Section 4 allows 28 days of detention without charge since 2012 — replacing the ISA but retaining preventive-detention powers.
cards.sub: The 28-day window can be used while authorities decide whether to charge. The 28% release-without-charge figure shows how often the answer is 'no'.

cards.big: SUHAKAM has run public inquiries into custodial conditions — most recently a 28-day inquiry into torture and death in custody at Taiping Prison.
cards.sub: Custodial-death inquiries provide one of the few public windows into how preventive detention functions in practice.

cards.big: Suaram's annual reports remain the most consistent civil-society source on SOSMA usage — official Home Ministry figures are not released in disaggregated form.
cards.sub: The asymmetry between civil-society reporting and government disclosure is itself a structural transparency gap.

cards.big: SOSMA is no longer a rare emergency tool — it is a routine pre-charge detention regime that releases one in four detainees uncharged.
Like a 28-day holding cell where one in four occupants is released with no charge — and there is no public log of who entered or why.
cards.sub: Pre-charge detention used at scale, with limited public accountability for the cases that never produce charges.

cards.big: Genuine SOSMA reform requires disaggregated public reporting on arrests, durations, charges and outcomes — not just renewal debates every five years.
